sanskrit: त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः । कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥
hindiTranslation: जो कर्म और फलकी आसक्तिका त्याग करके आश्रयसे रहित और सदा तृप्त है, वह कर्मोंमें अच्छी तरह लगा हुआ भी वास्तवमें कुछ भी नहीं करता ॥
englishTranslation: One who has abandoned attachment to the fruits of action, remains ever content, and depends on nothing, does nothing at all even while fully engaged in action.
essence: Action loses its grip when nothing in you waits to possess its fruit.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person can stay fully active and still be inwardly free by dropping attachment to results and resting in inner contentment.
simpleInsight: You can give yourself completely without being owned by the outcome.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna now explains the hidden freedom of action: the wise person acts, yet inwardly clings to nothing, so the action leaves no binding trace.
modernRelevance: You send the message, make the call, or resign from the safe job — and then you stop chasing the reaction. The clean act is enough.
translationLiteral: त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / कर्मफलासङ्गम् (attachment to the fruit of action) / नित्यतृप्तः (ever content) / निराश्रयः (without dependence) / कर्मणि (in action) / अभिप्रवृत्तः अपि (even while fully engaged) / न एव (indeed not) / किञ्चित् (anything) / करोति (does) / सः (he)
insight: It shifts attention from ownership of results to freedom within action.

quoteVariants.text: Act fully. The doer is already free.
The action is happening, but nothing is being clung to.
